samples.reference: Bajaj Hindusthan, India's largest sugar manufacturer, has modified its draft merger scheme to merge only Bajaj Eco-Tec Products with itself, excluding Bajaj Aviation. This decision was announced in a BSE filing, where the company did not provide a reason for the exclusion of Bajaj Aviation, which is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Bajaj Eco-Tec. The original merger plan, discussed on March 13, 2012, aimed to consolidate operations and reduce costs. Bajaj Eco-Tec Products produces particle boards for the construction industry, while Bajaj Aviation operates one leased aircraft. Following the announcement, Bajaj Hindusthan's shares fell by 3.15% to Rs 30.70. The company operates 14 sugar mills with a cane crushing capacity of 136,000 tonnes per day.

samples.source: Jammu/Srinagar : At least 12 civilians and a soldier were killed and another 42 people injured in Jammu and Kashmir’s Poonch district as the Pakistan army resorted to intense artillery shelling along the Line of Control (LoC) early on Wednesday, triggering panic among local residents and distress evacuations from border villages and hamlets. The shelling came hours after Indian forces carried out precision strikes on nine terror targets in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, in retaliation f
samples.reference: Tensions between India and Pakistan escalated sharply on Wednesday as intense Pakistani artillery shelling along the Line of Control (LoC) in Jammu and Kashmir’s Poonch district killed at least 12 civilians and one Indian soldier, while 42 others were injured. The shelling followed India’s Operation Sindoor, a series of precision strikes on nine terrorist targets in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) in response to the April 22 Pahalgam terror attack, which killed 26 civilians. The deceased include four children and members of the Sikh and Muslim communities. Several villages such as Mendhar, Balakot, and Krishna Ghati were hit, leading to mass panic and evacuation. Underground bunkers were used, and dozens of homes were damaged. Similar shelling incidents and injuries were reported from Uri, Kupwara, and Tangdhar in north Kashmir. Indian authorities, including Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha and Chief Minister Omar Abdullah, condemned the attacks and assured full state support. The Indian Army confirmed it is responding “appropriately” and “calibratedly” to the ceasefire violations. This marks the most serious LoC escalation since the 2021 ceasefire agreement. Analysts see it as a deliberate Pakistani move to retaliate and provoke after India's air and ground operations against terror camps, further destabilizing border security.
samples.generated: At least 12 civilians and a soldier were killed and another 42 people injured in Jammu and Kashmir’s Poonch district as the Pakistan army resorted to intense artillery shelling along the Line of Control (LoC) early on Wednesday, triggering panic among local residents and distress evacuations from border villages and hamlets. The shelling came hours after Indian forces carried out precision strikes on nine terror targets in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir, in retaliation for the Pahalgam terror attack that killed 26 people two weeks ago. The Indian Army said it was responding in an effective and appropriate manner. The indiscriminate shelling created panic

samples.reference: iGate Corporation has announced an offer of Rs 520 per share to shareholders of Patni Computer Systems as part of its plan to delist the Indian company from stock exchanges. This price, determined through a reverse book building process, was accepted in accordance with SEBI regulations. The offer opened on March 28 and closed on March 30. iGate CEO Phaneesh Murthy stated that the price offers a reasonable premium for Patni shareholders while remaining beneficial for iGate. Following a successful acquisition of a majority stake in Patni for USD 1.2 billion, iGate aims to complete the delisting by mid-2012. Current shareholders will have the opportunity to sell their shares at the discovered price for one year after delisting. iGate holds approximately 82% of Patni and is looking to streamline operations and reduce compliance costs through this move.

samples.reference: Coolex 2012, a four-day exhibition organized by the CII Northern Region, concluded at Himachal Bhawan, attracting a record 30,000 visitors. The event showcased a variety of air conditioners and refrigerators, featuring brands like Mitsubishi Electric, Voltas, and Samsung. Domestic air conditioner prices ranged from Rs 16,000 to Rs 38,000, while commercial units started at Rs 45,000. Notable products included the Tornado cooler, which was launched at the event, and Usha's new cooler range developed in collaboration with Honeywell. Samsung and Godrej introduced 5-star rated refrigerators, while LG presented its high-capacity "Door-in-Door" model. The exhibition offered attractive financing options for buyers, reinforcing its reputation as a key marketplace for cooling solutions.

samples.source: The rupee today recovered most of its early losses but still ended 3 paise down at 51.14/15 against the US currency due to late dollar selling by exporters.The rupee resumed sharply lower at 51.37/38 a dollar at the Interbank Foreign Exchange (Forex) market from last close of 51.11/12. It immediately touched a low of 51.45.Dealers attributed early steep weakness in the rupee to sluggish equities amid heavy dollar demand from importers.However,late dollar selling by exporters helped the rupee rec
samples.reference: The Indian rupee experienced fluctuations in today's trading, ultimately closing 3 paise lower at 51.14/15 against the US dollar. It began the day significantly weaker at 51.37/38, hitting a low of 51.45 due to high dollar demand from importers amid sluggish equity markets. However, late selling of dollars by exporters helped the rupee recover some losses. The BSE Sensex fell nearly 264 points, or 1.51 percent. Analysts noted that the dollar index, which had been strong, lost some ground, contributing to the rupee's recovery. The rupee's forward premium increased, with six-month contracts rising to 172-174 paise. The Reserve Bank of India set the reference rate for the US dollar at 51.2765, while the rupee improved against the euro but weakened against the Japanese yen.
samples.generated: The Indian rupee recovered most of its early losses but still ended 3 paise down at 51.14/15 against the US currency due to late dollar selling by exporters. Last Wednesday,it had fallen by 39 paise or 0.77 per cent. “The dollar index which was trading at day high pared some of its gains towards mid-session which aided the rupee to recover some of the lost grounds,” Pramit Brahmbhatt,CEO,Alpari Financial Services (India) said.

samples.reference: Government securities (G-Sec) saw an increase in demand from banks and corporates, leading to higher prices and lower yields across various maturities. The 9.15% G-Sec maturing in 2024 rose to Rs 103.4850, with its yield decreasing to 8.69%. Similarly, the 8.79% G-Sec maturing in 2021 went up to Rs 101.0350, yielding 8.63%. Other G-Secs also experienced price increases and yield declines. In contrast, the overnight call rate rose to 8.80% from 7.50% due to a lack of supply in the banking system, fluctuating between 9.05% and 8.80%. The Reserve Bank of India conducted transactions under the Liquidity Adjustment Facility, purchasing securities worth Rs 1,06,585 crore at an 8.50% repo rate and selling Rs 10 crore at a 7.50% reverse repo rate.

samples.source: In order to absorb excess liquidity,the Reserve Bank has pegged the quantum of intervention through Market Stabilisation Scheme (MSS) at Rs 50,000 crore.Under the MSS,RBI,on behalf of government,absorbs liquidity by issuing Treasury Bills and/ or dated securities.“The ceiling for outstanding balance under the MSS for the fiscal year 2012-13 has been fixed at Rs 50,000 crore,” the RBI said in a statement.The MSS ceiling is the same as was fixed for last fiscal. “This ceiling will be reviewed when
samples.reference: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has set the Market Stabilisation Scheme (MSS) intervention limit at Rs 50,000 crore for the fiscal year 2012-13, the same as the previous year. The MSS allows the RBI to absorb excess liquidity by issuing Treasury Bills or dated securities on behalf of the government. The ceiling will be reassessed when the outstanding balance reaches Rs 35,000 crore. The MSS was established in 2004 to manage liquidity resulting from significant foreign exchange inflows. For the current fiscal year, the government's borrowing requirement is pegged at Rs 5.13 lakh crore, with approximately 65% expected to be borrowed in the first half of the year. In the previous fiscal year, government borrowing exceeded its target due to high subsidy expenditures.
samples.generated: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has set the quantum of intervention through the Market Stabilisation Scheme (MSS) at Rs 50,000 crore. Under the MSS, the government absorbs excess liquidity by issuing Treasury Bills and/or dated securities. The MSS ceiling is the same as was fixed for the previous fiscal year, and will be reviewed when the outstanding balance reaches the threshold limit of Rs 35,000 crores. The government launched MSS in consultation with RBI in 2004 with the objective to absorb excess liquidity,arising out of significant foreign exchange inflows,by issuing treasury bills or dated securities

Standard gold (99.5% purity) rose by Rs 90 to Rs 27,985 per 10 grams, while pure gold (99.9% purity) increased by Rs 95 to Rs 28,115. samples.reference: A new study from Brown University indicates that prenatal methamphetamine exposure may lead to behavioral problems in children. Previous research has linked meth use during pregnancy to issues such as stunted fetal growth and increased stress levels in newborns. The recent study found that children exposed to meth in utero are more likely to exhibit anxiety and depression by age three and display behavioral issues, including symptoms of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD), by age five. Researcher Linda LaGasse emphasized the long-term implications of these early behaviors, suggesting they could set the stage for future challenges unless addressed. The study analyzed data from mothers who reported meth use during pregnancy and assessed their children’s behaviors through follow-up interviews. The findings raise concerns about the potential lasting effects of meth exposure on child development.

samples.reference: An Iranian music band, Barobax Corp, has filed a lawsuit in the Bombay High Court seeking to halt the release of the upcoming spy thriller 'Agent Vinod,' starring Saif Ali Khan and Kareena Kapoor, due to alleged copyright infringement. The band claims that the song "Pungi bajaa de" from the film is copied from their 2010 composition, "Soosan Khanoom." After sending a legal notice to the film's music director and distributors on March 16 with no response, Barobax is now requesting the court to prevent the movie's release and the use of the disputed song. The high court is expected to hear the case soon. Barobax, founded in 2003 by three Iranian nationals, registered the copyright for their song in Canada in June 2022.

samples.source: Telecom major Vodafone today said the government’s move to retrospectively amend income tax laws does not augur well for investors but asserted that it has no plan to exit India.“As a practising businessman in India,this will not augur well for foreign direct investment and the country at large,” Vodafone India’s non-executive Chairman Analjit Singh said in response to a query on the proposal to amend tax laws.Earlier in the day,a Finance Ministry official said: “They (Vodafone) will have to aut
samples.reference: Vodafone has expressed concerns regarding the Indian government's proposal to retrospectively amend income tax laws, stating that it could deter foreign direct investment. Analjit Singh, Vodafone India’s non-executive Chairman, emphasized that such changes are detrimental to the business environment and investor confidence. The proposed amendment, which aims to tax overseas deals involving domestic assets from 1962, directly affects Vodafone following a Supreme Court ruling in its favor regarding an ₹11,000 crore tax claim related to its 2007 acquisition of Hutchison Essar. Despite these challenges, Singh affirmed that Vodafone has no plans to exit the Indian market, highlighting the company's commitment to the opportunities in India. The proposal has drawn criticism from various corporate leaders, including the US-India Business Council President, who noted the negative implications for investor sentiment.

samples.reference: The Gujarat Congress has won the Mansa Assembly constituency, ending over 21 years of BJP dominance in the area. This victory is significant as Mansa is considered a BJP stronghold and was closely monitored by Chief Minister Narendra Modi. The by-election was called following the death of former speaker Mangaldas Patel, with Congress candidate Babusinh Thakor defeating BJP's Dashrath Patel by 8,182 votes. The turnout was around 75%. This loss marks a second setback for the BJP in north Gujarat, following a previous defeat in the Gandhinagar Municipal Corporation. BJP officials attributed the loss to a lack of a multi-party contest, while Congress leaders, including Shankersinh Vaghela, celebrated the win as a pivotal moment ahead of upcoming Assembly elections. The Nationalist Congress Party also expressed its desire for collaboration with Congress to strengthen efforts against the BJP.

The situation arose after Anurup Bhattacharya, the children's father, filed for legal separation from his wife, Sagarika, citing domestic abuse. Following the public fallout, Minister of State for External Affairs Preneet Kaur stated that the government would not interfere in the family matter, despite previously taking a strong stance by issuing multiple official statements and engaging with the Norwegian government. The visit of a Joint Secretary to Norway has been postponed as the government reassesses the situation, emphasizing the need for caution and a focus on family dynamics rather than diplomatic interventions.

samples.reference: Prime Minister Manmohan Singh has convened an all-party meeting on Friday to address the ongoing stalemate over the Lokpal Bill, which has received 97 amendments from Opposition members in the Rajya Sabha. The meeting, scheduled for noon at Singh's residence, aims to build consensus on the contentious amendments, including proposals to bring the Prime Minister’s Office under Lokpal, exclude Lokayukta from the bill, and include the Leader of Opposition in the selection committee. The government is eager to pass the Lokpal Bill in the Rajya Sabha, especially as social activist Anna Hazare plans to fast from March 25 if it is not enacted. The Lok Sabha has already approved the bill, and the government is considering dropping certain clauses to facilitate its passage before the budget session concludes on March 30.

samples.reference: B.S. Yeddyurappa is poised to return as Chief Minister of Karnataka after months of pressure on BJP leadership. He was summoned for discussions with key BJP figures, including Nitin Gadkari, L.K. Advani, and Arun Jaitley, amid expectations that a core committee meeting would confirm his reinstatement. This development follows the BJP's significant loss in the Udupi-Chikmagalur Lok Sabha bypoll, highlighting Yeddyurappa's continued importance to the party, particularly among the Lingayat community, which constitutes 18% of the electorate. Positive signals regarding his return were reportedly communicated to Yeddyurappa by Gadkari and Jaitley, leading him to end his boycott of the Karnataka Assembly budget session. Jaitley has been tasked with reviewing pending legal issues involving Yeddyurappa and his family, with indications that the charges may not be substantial.

Lok Sabha Speaker Meira Kumar has reached out to Tendulkar regarding the recognition, although specific details are still being finalized. This acknowledgment follows recent praise from both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha, celebrating Tendulkar's historic accomplishment during a one-day international match against Bangladesh on March 16, 2012. The article also briefly mentions a separate legislative bill that has garnered mixed reactions across various sectors, with benefits for some groups while negatively impacting others, particularly low-income Americans and certain industries.
samples.generated: Plans are underway for felicitating Sachin Tendulkar in Parliament over his feat of completing a century of centuries. Lok Sabha Speaker Meira Kumar has written a letter to him on the issue but the details are yet to be worked out,Parliament sources said. Just two days back,both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha had lauded the cricketer for becoming the first cricketers to score a hundred centuries in international cricket. The Speaker had said the outstanding batsman achieved this incredible feat during the one day international match against Bangladesh at Mirpur on March 16,2012.

samples.source: Steps to strengthen nuclear security and measures to prevent terrorists getting access to atomic weapons will top the agenda of 58 world leaders,including Prime Minister Manmohan Singh,in Seoul next week.Singh will embark on a four-day visit to South Korea on Saturday to participate in the second Nuclear Security Summit (NSS) and strengthen ties with the Asian economic partner.“The main objective of the Summit has been high-level attention on the global threat posed by nuclear terrorism and the
samples.reference: Next week, 58 world leaders, including Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, will convene in Seoul for the second Nuclear Security Summit (NSS), focusing on enhancing nuclear security and preventing terrorist access to atomic weapons. Singh's four-day visit aims to strengthen ties with South Korea, highlighted by his participation in the summit. Foreign Secretary Ranjan Mathai emphasized the summit's objective of addressing the global threat of nuclear terrorism and reviewing progress since the first NSS in Washington in 2010. Countries will present national progress reports on nuclear security measures implemented since then. Singh's attendance underscores India's commitment to the summit's goals, marking the first visit by an Indian Prime Minister to South Korea since 1993.
samples.generated: Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh is set to visit South Korea to participate in the second Nuclear Security Summit (NSS) and strengthen ties with the Asian economic partner. The summit aims to address the global threat posed by nuclear terrorism and the measures required to prevent terrorists and non-state actors from gaining access to sensitive nuclear materials and technologies. The NSS is intended to assess progress in implementing the Communique and Work Plan of the first Summit held in Washington in 2010, as well as agree on new measures that would be reflected in the Summit Communique”,Mathai said.

samples.reference: Sahara India Pariwar honored the Indian hockey team and its support staff with a cash award of Rs 1.16 crore for their success in the FIH Olympic qualifiers, which secured their qualification for the London Olympics. The ceremony, led by Sahara India chief Subrata Roy, recognized standout players Sardar Singh and Sandeep Singh with Rs 11 lakh each, while other team members and chief coach Michael Nobbs received Rs 5 lakh each. Support staff members were awarded Rs 1 lakh each. Roy expressed pride in the team's spirit and commitment, while Hockey India Secretary General Narendra Batra emphasized that this recognition would inspire the team to maintain their dedication and performance.

The players and support staff members were honored with the award and gifts by Sahara India chief Subrata Roy in a glittering ceremony here. For their outstanding display,Sardar Singh and Sandeep Singh were awarded Rs. 11 lakhs each,while the rest of the team members along with the chief coach Michael Nobbs were given Rs. 5 lakh each for their performance.

samples.reference: The Karnataka High Court's decision to quash an FIR against former Chief Minister B.S. Yeddyurappa has been challenged in the Supreme Court by the original complainant, Srijan Basha. Basha filed a Special Leave Petition (SLP), asserting that he was not included in the high court proceedings and that the ruling, made on March 7, was issued without his input. He contends that the court's quashing of the Governor's sanction for Yeddyurappa's prosecution is legally flawed and contradicts established legal precedents. The high court had previously deemed a section of the Lokayukta report that implicated Yeddyurappa in illegal mining as "bad in law," which contributed to his resignation as chief minister.

samples.reference: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has requested Interpol to issue look-out notices for four Iranian nationals linked to a bomb attack on an Israeli embassy car in New Delhi. The individuals, Houshang Afshar Irani, Syed Ali Mahdiansadr, Mohammadreza Abolghasemi, and Masoud Sedaghatzadeh, are suspected of conducting reconnaissance on the embassy prior to the attack, which occurred on February 13 and injured four people. Sedaghatzadeh was arrested in Malaysia, and investigators believe he had connections with an Indian journalist, Syed Ahemed Kazmi, who was also arrested. The CBI, acting as India's nodal agency for Interpol, aims to secure a Red Corner Notice, which functions similarly to an international arrest warrant, to facilitate the extradition of the suspects.

samples.source: A Bangladesh court today ordered authorities to shut down five Facebook pages and a website for blaspheming the Prophet Mohammed,the Quran and other religious subjects,a lawyer said.Judges at the high court in Dhaka ordered the telecommunications regulator,home ministry officials and police to block the offending pages immediately.“These pages contain disparaging remarks and cartoons about Prophet Mohammed,the Holy book,Jesus,Lord Buddha and Hindu gods,” Nawshad Zamir,a lawyer of the petitioner
samples.reference: A court in Dhaka, Bangladesh, has ordered the shutdown of five Facebook pages and a website for blaspheming the Prophet Mohammed, the Quran, and other religious figures. The ruling came after a joint petition from a Dhaka University teacher and a law school head, citing that the pages contained offensive remarks and cartoons that hurt the sentiments of the majority Muslim population and followers of other religions. The court instructed telecommunications regulators, home ministry officials, and police to block the pages immediately. This marks the first instance of a Bangladesh court taking action against Facebook pages for religious offense. The move is part of a broader crackdown on internet use in the country, following previous incidents involving arrests for online comments about government officials.

samples.source: Assets under management of the New Pension System are likely to touch Rs 20,000 crore by the end of 2012 from Rs 14,000 crore at present,a top official said today.“AUM (Assets Under Management) of the NPS (New Pension System) are likely to move up from Rs 14,000 crore now to Rs 20,000 crore by the year-end,” NPS Trust Managing Trustee and Chief Executive Officer N R Rayalu said.The NPS trust was set up in 2008 by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) for taking care of th
samples.reference: The New Pension System (NPS) is projected to increase its assets under management (AUM) from Rs 14,000 crore to Rs 20,000 crore by the end of 2012, according to NPS Trust Managing Trustee N R Rayalu. Established in 2008 by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA), the NPS aims to manage pension assets for subscribers. Rayalu emphasized the potential of pension funds to significantly contribute to infrastructure development, as India plans to invest Rs 50 lakh crore during the 12th Five Year Plan (2012-2017). Currently, only 13% of the workforce is covered by pension schemes, highlighting the need for increased financial education and inclusion of the unorganized sector in social security.

samples.source: NEW DELHI : Twenty-seven airports across northern India were closed for commercial flights until Saturday morning, following India’s targeted strikes in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir early on Wednesday. India also shut 25 international flight routes that allow aircraft to enter Indian airspace and overfly Pakistan.

Additionally, 25 international air routes that involve entry into Indian airspace and overflight through Pakistan were suspended as a precautionary security measure.

samples.source: Tata Motors-owned Jaguar Land Rover today said it has finalised Chery Automobile Company as partner to form an equal joint venture in China that will produce both JLR and the JV-branded vehicles.The new JV entity will also manufacture engines and set up a research and development centre in China.“Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) and Chery Automobile Company Ltd have reached agreement on a proposed joint venture in China.The agreement follows extensive talks between JLR and Chery on establishing an equal
samples.reference: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) has finalized an equal joint venture with Chery Automobile Company in China to produce vehicles under both JLR and a new JV brand. The partnership will also involve engine manufacturing and the establishment of a research and development center in China. JLR CEO Ralf Speth and Chery Chairman Yin Tongyao expressed optimism about the collaboration, highlighting the growing demand for JLR vehicles in China, the world's largest car market. The joint venture aims to leverage both companies' strengths in R&D and manufacturing to meet local consumer needs. Although specific terms of the agreement remain undisclosed, the venture is projected to require regulatory approvals for an investment of 17.5 billion yuan (approximately USD 2.78 billion) and could initially produce 50,000 vehicles, employing around 5,000 people in Wuhu, Anhui Province.
samples.generated: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) and Chery Automobile Company Ltd have reached agreement on a proposed joint venture in China. The scope of the proposed JV will include the manufacture of JLR and JV-branded vehicles, establishment of a R&D facility,engine manufacturing and sale of vehicles produced by the JV company,it added. The statement further said JLR will now follow the official process to establish a JV in China in the coming months.

samples.source: Outlining six norms for investment in capital market by Muslims,Islamic seminary Darul Uloom Deoband has said followers can invest in the equity of a company which is not involved in production of liquor and interest-based businesses.Mufti Fakhrul Islam while issuing a fatwa said Muslims can invest in those companies which have movable as well as immovable properties.The seminary said that a part of the profit should be distributed among the poor. Besides,it has also advocated for taking informe
samples.reference: Darul Uloom Deoband, an Islamic seminary, has outlined six guidelines for Muslim investment in the capital market, emphasizing that investments should not involve companies associated with liquor or interest-based businesses. Mufti Fakhrul Islam stated that Muslims can invest in companies with both movable and immovable assets, provided a portion of the profits is shared with the poor. Investors are encouraged to make informed decisions and to voice concerns at shareholder meetings if they discover that a company engages in interest-related activities. The seminary also warned against investing in companies focused solely on profit maximization. This guidance comes amid ongoing discussions about the potential for Islamic banking practices in India, which could attract significant investments from the Middle East, despite some judicial resistance to such institutions in the country.
samples.generated: Islamic seminary Darul Uloom Deoband has announced six norms for investment in the capital market by Muslims. Mufti Fakhrul Islam emphasized the need for movable and immovable properties to be distributed among the poor. The seminary also advocated for informed decisions before making investments in the equity market. An investor is required to register protest in the shareholders meeting if they come to know that the company earns or pay interests for doing its business,Mufti said. It has also cautioned that people should not invest in those companies whose sole objective is profit-maximisation.

verdict: ✅ GOOD — Model is mostly factually consistent
